prediction.attackType: MITRE T1078.004 Valid Accounts abuse by privileged admin
prediction.summary: A privileged user, 'admin_jones', engaged in highly suspicious off-hours activity. This included deleting a user, flushing firewall rules, accessing the '/etc/shadow' file, stopping the audit daemon, and clearing command history. These actions strongly indicate potential account compromise and an attempt to evade detection and maintain persistence.

timeline.event: User 'temp_contractor' deleted by a privileged user.
Firewall rules flushed by a privileged user.
Audit daemon stopped by a privileged user.
admin_jones initiated an off-hours privileged session from 198.18.0.9.
SIEM alert: Suspicious admin activity by admin_jones (firewall flush, user delete, audit stop).
SIEM alert: admin_jones activity detected outside normal working hours (Mon-Fri 09:00-17:00).
SIEM identified the activity as MITRE T1078.004 Valid Accounts abuse by privileged admin.

recommendations: Immediately disable or suspend the 'admin_jones' account to prevent further unauthorized actions.
Initiate a full forensic investigation of the affected system and the 'admin_jones' account for signs of compromise, including malware, backdoors, or other persistence mechanisms.
Analyze all logs from source IP '198.18.0.9' for additional malicious activity or connections to other internal/external systems.
Determine the full impact of the 'userdel' command and restore the 'temp_contractor' user if necessary, investigating why it was deleted.
Review and restore firewall rules to their baseline configuration, ensuring network security is not compromised.
Investigate why auditd was stopped and ensure it is re-enabled and functioning correctly to maintain logging integrity.
Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all privileged accounts and enforce strict access controls for off-hours activity.
